description: Schema round-trip fixtures (spec 006, FR-003/009/010/011). Each binding feeds the document at `path` through its own LOADER matching `form` (load_json/loadJson or load_yaml/loadYaml) and MUST reach the expected `verdict` identically: an `accept` doc loads cleanly; a `reject` doc raises the binding's normalized structured error (no partial load, no crash). Reuses the existing schemas/jsonschema/tests/fixtures set (does not fork it; relocated under tests/ in spec 008) plus one valid + one invalid YAML twin for the load_yaml path. Paths are repo-relative and MUST resolve within the repo root (SEC-001). NOTE ON LAYERS: the loaders do serde SHAPE deserialization, not full JSON-Schema validation. Two of the schema `invalid/` fixtures encode JSON-Schema `propertyNames`/structural rules that serde cannot model and are enforced DOWNSTREAM (by check()/render), so the loaders accept them: `variant-named-default` (a variant literally named 'default' — flagged by check() as a ReservedVariantName finding) is therefore EXCLUDED from this loader round-trip set. (`variant-redefines-role` IS loader-rejected because the Variant struct is additionalProperties:false over {body,meta}, so a `role` key fails serde deserialization — it is included.) The corpus tests the loader's verdict, which is the binding-observable round-trip; the schema-validator's stricter verdict is spec-001's `validate-fixtures` gate.

fixtures.note: missing a required field (serde: missing field)
role not in enum (serde: unknown variant)
trusted field has wrong type (serde: expected bool, got string)
a variant redefining role (serde: Variant is additionalProperties:false)
YAML twin of bad-role (load_yaml reject path)
